title: Mastery: full paragraph at 92 WPM
text: Mastery is quiet. The typist at ninety-two words per minute does not look fast. The hands barely move. The keyboard makes a steady, even sound. What you are seeing is the absence of effort — years of practice compressed into a few quick, certain gestures. That is what you have built over the past ninety lessons.

tips.text: Block 7 is the final polish on a skill you built slowly, a little at a time. This is the quiet part of the craft.
Ninety-two should feel calm now, not strained. If your shoulders are up around your ears, you are pushing too hard. Let them drop.

tips.target: Mastery is quiet. The typist at ninety-two words per minute does not look fast. The hands barely move. The keyboard makes a steady, even sound. What you are

tips.text: If you are fighting for the speed, ease off and let the accuracy lead. Speed at this level is what good accuracy turns into on its own.

tips.target: seeing is the absence of effort — years of practice compressed into a few quick, certain gestures. That is what you have built over the past ninety lessons.

character_tips.anna: Block 7. Mastery is not effort piled higher; it is effort that has disappeared into the hands.
character_tips.maxim: Nine lessons remain, and each one buffs what you already own. Nothing new to learn now, only to settle.
character_tips.knopych: The summit is in sight. Keep your pace honest and you will be standing on it soon.
character_tips.klavochka: The final ascent. Go steady and graceful; the quiet way is the fast way now. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
